questions.text: Finish the trace above: what value replaces 30 in the tree?
questions.explanation: 30's right subtree contains only 35, which has no left child, so 35 is already the leftmost node there and is the in-order successor. 30's value is overwritten with 35, and the now-duplicate leaf node holding 35 is removed.

questions.text: After deleting 30 this way, what happens to node 25 and its right child 27?
choices: Both move to become children of the new 35 node
Neither moves; 25 stays exactly where it was, as the left child of the node now holding 35
questions.explanation: Only the node holding 30's replacement value changes; the rest of the subtree is untouched. 25 (with its right child 27 still attached) stays as the left child of whichever node now holds the value 35.
